These trips are FREE to UNLV students, but interested students must apply to attend by completing the application form prior to 9 am on Monday, March 2. Questions? Email altbreaks@unlv.edu

name: "Interpersonal Communication: Say What You Mean"
description: In this interactive workshop, participants explore three critical aspects of effective communication. First, we focus on bridging the gap between the intended message, the actual delivery, and the recipient's perception by providing valuable tips to ensure clear and successful communication. Then we explore how nonverbal cues, like tone, body language, and timing, can enhance or undermine a message. Finally, we explore the pros and cons of email communication, uncovering potential pitfalls and benefits. Each segment includes group discussions and role plays to help participants incorporate the concepts into daily practice.

This session will be hosted by Aparna Polavarapu, professor of law and co-director of the Saltman Center of Conflict Resolution at William S. Boyd School of Law. Reflective Letter Writing Workshop: Following the discussion, the Care Center will transition into a creative workshop. Participants will be invited to write a letter to their past or future selves, or to someone else, as a way to externalize feelings and set intentions for the future. Help create a collaborative artwork that will be exhibited at the Barrick Museum! Date: Wednesday, March 25 | 1–3:30 PM Location: Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art Cost: Free and open to the public Follow us on Instagram for more updates: @carecenter_snv

name: Caribbean Students Association~ Sounds of the Caribbean: Leadership & Activism through Conscious Reggae
description: Join us for Sounds of the Caribbean, a celebration of artists as community leaders and the powerful role of conscious reggae music in shaping political awareness, social change, and community identity across the Black diaspora. This program highlights Bob Marley, whose music and message of unity, resistance, and empowerment continue to inspire communities worldwide. Through his songs and those of other conscious reggae artists, participants will explore how music serves as a tool for leadership—uplifting voices, challenging injustice, and guiding communities toward collective action. The event also spotlights unsung leaders of the Caribbean diaspora, musicians and cultural storytellers whose contributions may not always be widely recognized but have profoundly influenced their communities. From calypso to soca and reggae, Caribbean musical traditions demonstrate that leadership extends beyond formal roles—it emerges through creativity, cultural expression, and the power of shared stories. Come discover how rhythm, storytelling, and conscious music create movements, build communities, and inspire change.

name: Leadership Link Up ~ Leading Through Change - Shinobi Style
description: Just like Naruto grew from an underdog academy student to a respected Hokage, true leaders learn to adapt, persevere, and guide their squads through every mission—no matter how unpredictable the path. Whether you’re navigating personal growth arcs or helping your team move through major transitions, this session will help you tap into your inner ninja and lead with resilience, empathy, and clarity. Participants will: Recognize the “Shinobi Cycle of Change” — understanding how emotional phases (from confusion to clarity) affect individuals and entire squads. Reflect on personal adaptability by identifying your own strengths, blind spots, and “chakra drains” when facing transformation. Develop leadership strategies inspired by shinobi teamwork—learning how to support others with empathy, communicate clearly, and guide your team through uncertain missions. ​

name: Donut Worry, We Care! Care Center Open House

description: The Care Center warmly invites all students, faculty, and staff to our open house! S top by to grab free donuts, tour our facilities, meet our team, and learn about the wide range of services we offer to support students, faculty, and staff impacted by domestic violence, relationship violence, sexual violence, or stalking. All attendees will also be entered into an opportunity drawing for an exciting giveaway! This open house is the perfect chance to ask questions, discover ways to get involved during Sexual Assault Awareness Month, and connect with the Care Center team, whether you’re curious about our resources or want to learn about ways to support your community.

name: VIRTUAL Success Series: Bouncing back from Burnout
description: College can get overwhelming, and burnout is real. In Bouncing Back from Burnout, students will learn how to recognize burnout, manage stress in healthier ways, and build habits that support both their personal well-being and academic success. This workshop will provide practical tools, self-care tips, and helpful campus resources to help students feel more balanced and supported.

name: Human Trafficking Prevention Club Meeting
description: The Human Trafficking Prevention Club at UNLV invites you to join us for our Spring 2026 Meetings. These meetings provide a safe and supportive space to learn about human trafficking, understand the warning signs, and explore how you can help prevent it within your community. Throughout the semester, we will engage in open discussions, educational activities, and guest speaker conversations focused on awareness, prevention, and advocacy. Whether you are new to this topic or already passionate about social justice, all are welcome! Our goal is to empower individuals with knowledge, encourage meaningful dialogue, and inspire action against human trafficking.

Come connect with others, share some laughs, and enjoy the tasty rewards of your creations together! Registration is required and the form can be found in our Discord.

name: Field Trip: St. Thomas, NV
description: Join the Anthropology Society for a fun and educational walk about St. Thomas, a once-thriving community before Lake Mead was created! Enjoy a guided walk through the ruins of the town with Park Rangers who know a thing or two about the history of this unique feature in the Nevada desert. Be prepared to spend hours outdoors - bring plenty of water, good hiking shoes, sunscreen, hats, and a lunch. Rides can be provided by the UNLV Anthropology Department if we have a headcount by March 20, 2026. Cars will leave UNLV no later than 8:15 am, so please be on time!

name: Sikolohiyang Pilipino: Filipino Psychology
organization: UNLV Wellness Zone and Counseling and Psychological Services (CAPS)
description: Have you ever heard of utang na loob? Walang hiya? Kapwa? Sikolohiyang Pilipino, or Filipino Psychology, has concepts rooted in indigenous practices and values that better capture the Filipino experience. Join us this Asian American History Month in an online workshop to learn more about its history and foundational concepts!

name: Conference Proposal Writing Workshop
description: Grad Ready Series: Elevate Your Research: Master the Conference Proposal From Abstract to Podium. Conference proposal submission season is coming up. Navigating the transition from research to presentation is a critical milestone in your academic career. BGSA and the Writing Center invite you to a specialized session focused on the nuances of writing winning conference proposals for graduate-level symposiums and national conferences. Why Attend? The Reviewer’s Perspective: Understand what selection committees look for in a competitive abstract. Strategic Framing: Learn how to articulate your methodology and significance within strict word counts. Disciplinary Clarity: Tailor your pitch to suit both niche and interdisciplinary audiences. 📍 Session Details Date: April 3rd, 2026 Time: 4:00pm - 5:00pm Location: Online Target Audience: Current Masters and Doctoral candidates across all disciplines. Pro Tip: Bring a current abstract or a project description you are working on, so that you can ask specific questions.

Open to all UNLV students, families, and the wider community, the event is FREE, the showcase highlights the unique talents and passions of our OTD students as they share projects, demonstrations, and activities that reflect the meaningful ways they engage in life and work. Attendees can expect an evening filled with energy, inspiration, and a sense of connection, as participants explore the diverse ways occupational therapy enhances everyday life. Whether you’re a student, family member, or community supporter, this event is a wonderful opportunity to experience the creativity, innovation, and dedication of our future occupational therapists in a lively and welcoming environment. Come celebrate the meaningful occupations that shape our lives! Please click this link to receive your free tickets: Showcase of Meaningful Occupations

name: It's Just Coffee: Athletics at University
description: It's Just Coffee is a series of meet-ups - approximately once a month - for students to practice their dialogue skills in a low-stakes environment. This event brings together diverse perspectives to dialogue about a complex topic through sharing of viewpoints, listening openly, and working to understand each other. The goal is not consensus, it is shared learning. No topic expertise is required to participate, and there will be a dialogue facilitator and topic guide to assist all participants. And of course, there will be coffee (and tea and snacks)! Participants in this session may engage questions such as, "How much should coaches get paid?" and "Should it be the university's responsibility to equitably fund sports?"

name: Serve & Lead Mini-Con 2026: Taking Flight
description: The Serve & Lead MiniCon is a one-day conference designed to help UNLV students connect their community engagement and leadership development experiences with the careers and callings that await them. 2026 MiniCon - Taking Flight This year’s theme - Taking Flight - symbolizes how small actions, intentional design, and supportive winds can lift students into their next chapter with confidence and direction. It is also fitting for our first attempt to get the Mini-Con off the ground. Just as a paper airplane is crafted, adjusted, and launched with purpose, the Mini-Con empowers students to shape their own trajectory. Through interactive workshops, panel discussions, and hands-on engagement opportunities, participants will explore how service, community involvement, and leadership roles become the folds and angles that give structure to their professional identities. Attendees will learn to articulate the transferable skills gained through service work and leadership activities, and understand how these experiences create lift in any workplace or avocation. Whether students are still experimenting with the best design, catching an updraft, or ready for takeoff, the Serve & Lead Mini-Con offers the tools, networks, and inspiration to support their ascent. Join us as we explore how serving others builds stronger leaders—and how your unique story can take flight into a future driven by purpose, passion, and impact. NOTE: UNLV students are encouraged to attend for as much of the event as possible, but are not required to stay the entire time as we understand many will have class, work, or other obligations. Please RSVP even if you are only able to attend a session or two - we will be happy to have you! Full Event Details and Session Descriptions Event Agenda at a Glance: Time Activity 9:00 - 9:20 am Check-in opens, breakfast, sign-up for service projects 9:20 - 9:30 am Welcome and Introducing Keynote 9:30 - 9:50 am Keynote Speaker 9:50 - 10 am Break/Transition Time 10 am - 11 am Service Project Breakouts 11 - 12 pm Lunch & Guest Panel 12 - 12:45 pm Leadership Breakouts 12:45 - 12:55 pm Break/Transition Time 12:55 - 1:40 pm Leadership Breakouts 1:40 - 1:45 pm Break/Transition Time 1:45 - 2 pm Opportunity Drawing and Closing

name: 2026 Intercollegiate Big Day Competition!
description: Join UNLV Birding, NSU Flight Club and CSN Birding Club to participate in the Spring Intercollegiate Bird Day Competition! This is a birding competition between different universities within the US! As the only Nevada representative we will be going around bird hot spots to see how many bird species we can discover. We will be traveling to the Clark County Wetlands Park early morning to catch all the migratory birds near a riparian environment then heading to the Henderson Bird Viewing Preserve to catch some exposed water fowl. Come learn about the species of Las Vegas and improve your birding skills with us! Binoculars are available to be borrowed! Before the event, please create an account on eBird and download the phone application. This will be the main way we keep track of attendance and bird species identified! Sign up and download here: https://ebird.org/home Take a peak at the trip report on the event! - https://ebird.org/tripreport/472119 Here are two more applications that can help you ID, be sure to download them before the event! iNaturalist: https://www.inaturalist.org/ Merlin Bird ID: https://merlin.allaboutbirds.org/ Please RSVP to this event by April 8th! We will be sending out waivers before the event that you will need to sign, and an information request about your eBird profile to add you to the report, so we can get straight into birding!

name: Mapping Your Future: Life and Career Design in Action
location: Room 101 - Beverly Rogers Literature and Law Building
description: This workshop introduces "Life Design," a proactive approach to building a fulfilling future, directly applying principles used by product designers. It means getting unstuck from the pressure of having a perfect plan and instead, learning how to prototype different possibilities for your career, relationships, and well-being. You'll gain tools to reframe challenges, embrace curiosity, and take small, intentional steps towards a life that truly resonates with you, no matter what major you're pursuing. description: We are so excited to announce our first ever Brain Health Research Pipeline Panel! Meet and speak with graduate students, laboratory technicians, post-doctoral researchers, and other faculty including Dr. Kinney himself from the Kinney Lab (otherwise known as the Pam Quirk Biomarker Discovery Laboratory). Holding numerous positions on campus, Dr. Jefferson Kinney serves as NURO's club advisor, founding chair of the Department of Brain Health, and co-director of the Pam Quirk Brain Health and Biomarker Laboratory! This event will begin with an informative career-oriented presentation on the neuroscience research pipeline followed by an open Q&A session, so bring your questions and your notebooks–any and all curiosity is welcomed and encouraged! At the end of the event, teams receive their original concept back, now evolved through collaboration, and deliver a final pitch. This event encourages creativity, adaptability, and entrepreneurial thinking while incorporating cross-cultural perspectives in business development.

name: From Inner Mongolia to Las Vegas: Searching for Human Universals Across Dueling Cultures
description: Drawing on decades of fieldwork and lived experiences, this informal talk reflects on what Dr. Jankowiak has learned studying cultural life in very different social worlds: north China, Mormon polygamous families, Las Vegas swingers, and mainstream American society. Moving between these settings raises a persistent anthropological question: how different are people across cultures, and where do common human patterns emerge? By revisiting these encounters, Dr. Jankowiak explores what comparative experience reveals about culture, intimacy, and the possibility of human universals.

description: 🌟 UNLV Women Veteran Retreat 🌟 Resiliency • Enlighten • Empower Calling All Women Veterans! The UNLV Rebel Vets invite women veterans from campus and the community to attend a special Women Veteran Retreat to celebrate the strength, accomplishments, and individuality of those who have served. This unique gathering will be held at the UNLV Tam Alumni Center, creating a welcoming space for female veteran students and alumni women veterans to connect, recharge, and build lasting relationships. This retreat is more than an event—it’s a chance to step away from the daily routine, strengthen resiliency, and empower one another through shared experiences. 💬 What to Expect ✨ Three inspiring women veteran guest speakers representing: Social Work Entrepreneurship Vet Center employment pathways 🎉 Games, raffles, and interactive activities 🎶 Music and social time 🥗 Refreshments provided 🤝 Networking and connection with fellow women veterans Participants will gain new perspectives, learn about career pathways, and share meaningful conversations with women who understand the journey of military service and transition. 🌺 A Space Designed for Women Veterans This event is a women veterans-only gathering to foster open dialogue, support, and authentic connection. 📅 Join Us Come ready for a day of learning, laughter, reflection, and community as we celebrate the powerful network of women veterans at UNLV and throughout Southern Nevada. Resiliency. Enlighten. Empower. 📲 RSVP below Hosted by UNLV Rebel Vets. See you there!

name: Spring Semester Bird Walk!

Bring your laptop, come ready to learn, and enjoy bagels while connecting with others interested in AI and data science.

name: Global Grads & Friends: End-of-Semester Coffee Meetup ☕🌍
description: Join us for a special end-of-semester event as we bring together graduate and international students to celebrate the close of another successful term! Whether you stop by for a quick chat or stay longer to connect, we look forward to celebrating with you!

name: VIRTUAL Success Series: Finishing Strong
description: Finish the semester with confidence! In this workshop, you’ll learn practical strategies to prepare for the final weeks, effectively review course material, manage your time, and reduce stress. Event Vision We’ll also share tips on staying motivated, prioritizing tasks, and maintaining balance during one of the busiest times of the semester. Plus, the Writing Center will be joining us to share their expertise, along with helpful tools and tricks for strengthening your writing and successfully completing final papers and assignments. Set yourself up for success, feel more in control of your workload, and finish the semester on a strong and confident note. Students will receive a Google Calendar invite to join the session. Please remember to accept the invite so it is saved to your calendar. We look forward to seeing you there!

Participants will be invited to share experiences and perspectives in an open dialogue setting. Open to everyone. 📅 April 27 | 1:30 PM–3:00 PM 📍 SU 228 RSVP Today!

If you have any questions or concerns regarding this event, please feel free to contact us at: carecenter@unlv.edu

name: Xiao Jiao Corner
description: 🐼 Xiao Jiao Corner – Practice Chinese Together 一起练习中文 | All Levels Welcome Looking for a relaxed and supportive space to practice Chinese? Join us at Xiao Jiao Corner, a welcoming environment where students of all proficiency levels can grow their language skills together. Whether you're just starting out or already conversational, this is a low-pressure space to build confidence and improve through practice. 🌟 What We’ll Do: Practice speaking Chinese in small groups Practice writing Chinese characters Receive peer feedback Ask questions before turning in assignments Connect with fellow Chinese language learners 💛 Why It Works: No pressure No judgment Learn at your own pace Supportive, student-led environment Come for one session or join us regularly — we’re here to help each other succeed!

name: Business Law Panel
description: Join us for an engaging afternoon dedicated to the world of business law. We are excited to host a panel of esteemed professionals who will share their unique career journeys, the lessons they’ve learned in the field, and their best advice for those looking to follow a similar path. This is more than just a presentation—it is your chance to hear real-world stories, ask questions during our Q&A session, and gain valuable insights into the legal industry. Our Featured Panelists: Lori Johnson (Professor of Law at UNLV Boyd School of Law) Erin Barnet (Vice President, Corporate Counsel at Fontainebleau Las Vegas) Austin K. F. Benner (Associate at Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani) Tyler Stevens (Associate at Greenberg Traurig) Kirche Ray (Associate at Greenberg Traurig). 📅 Date: April 27th 🕒 Time: 4:00 PM – 6:00 PM 📍 Location: HOS 384 This event is open to ALL students interested in law, business, or litigation (Membership is not required to attend). Questions? Email us at blawassociation@gmail.com. Stay Connected: 📱 IG: @Unlvbla

name: The Exchange: Where Opportunities Begin
description: A student-led event hosted by International Business Association and AI in Business, and sponsored by RebelBot. The Exchange brings together students, faculty, and professionals for meaningful conversations, new connections, and real opportunities. The goal is to create a space where people can connect across industries, share resources, explore internships and career paths, and build relationships that actually lead somewhere. What to expect: Networking and community building Guest speakers Open mic for opportunities, resources, and announcements A look at RebelBot Date: Monday, April 27 Time: 5:30 PM – 8:10 PM Location: Lee Business School, UNLV RSVP: https://luma.com/r0a2l5sd Open Mic Interest Form: https://forms.gle/bFBQH93iZkCXemjG6 Try RebelBot: https://rebelbot.ai

name: It's Just Coffee: Partisanship in American Politics
description: It's Just Coffee is a series of meet-ups - approximately once a month - for students to practice their dialogue skills in a low-stakes environment. This event brings together diverse perspectives to dialogue about a complex topic through sharing of viewpoints, listening openly, and working to understand each other. The goal is not consensus, it is shared learning. No topic expertise is required to participate, and there will be a dialogue facilitator and topic guide to assist all participants. And of course, there will be coffee (and tea and snacks)! Participants in this session may engage questions such as, "What do we have to give up to get the political system we want?" and "Should we reform politics and government to encourage compromise, or will that mean giving up on the changes we really need and want?"

name: Rainbow Story Hour
organization: UNLV Wellness Zone and Counseling and Psychological Services (CAPS)
description: Remember the good ole’ days of being a kid and heading to your favorite library for storytime? You're in luck! Join us for our first Rainbow Story Hour – storytime for LGBTQ+ students and Allies. Bring your favorite plushie, wear your most comfy clothes, and join us on the storytime rug for snacks, activities, and the books Not Quite Narwhal and Miss Rita, Mystery Reader.

name: Sikolohiyang Pilipino: Filipino Psychology
organization: UNLV Wellness Zone and Counseling and Psychological Services (CAPS)
description: Have you ever heard of utang na loob? Walang hiya? Kapwa? Sikolohiyang Pilipino, or Filipino Psychology, has concepts rooted in indigenous practices and values that better capture the Filipino experience. Join us this Asian American History Month in an online workshop to learn more about its history and foundational concepts!

description: This workshop offers a comprehensive overview of the graduate school application process and what to anticipate. We will address key considerations for program selection, understanding admission prerequisites, and developing compelling personal statements. Additionally, you will gain insights into funding opportunities, preparation strategies for standardized examinations, and cultivating strong professional relationships for recommendations. This session aims to equip you with the essential knowledge to confidently pursue your advanced degree aspirations. Register on Handshake: https://unlv.joinhandshake.com/events/1894874/share_preview

location: UNLV Student Union & Event Services: ROOM 209
description: Join us for a full day of connection, reflection, and community as we honor Denim Day on April 29. The Care Center is hosting a series of free activities throughout the day to support victim-survivors while promoting education, prevention, and a safer campus. Learn more about the meaning behind Denim Day and take part in spaces designed for healing, creativity, and conversation. Itinerary: 10 a.m. | Sips & Self-Discovery Start your day with a cup of coffee and guided journaling! Explore prompts centered on support, intention-setting, and meaningful moments of connection. In partnership with UNLV Project Wellness. 11 a.m. | Healing in Motion: Yoga Session Take a moment to reset and reconnect through a calming, guided yoga session. Grab free yoga session vouchers! 12 p.m. | Let’s Taco ’Bout Respect: Lunch & Trivia We'll be providing Chipotle during lunch while testing your knowledge on consent, healthy relationships, and bystander intervention. Prizes available for top participants. In partnership with Care Advocates. 1:30 p.m. | Art for Advocacy Engage in creative activities such as bracelet making, bookmark decor, button making, and more. Contribute to collaborative art pieces and learn more about Denim Day and Care Center resources. In partnership with the Marjorie Barrick Museum of Art. name: General Meeting
description: 🏗️ Building Our Future: EWB General Meeting! Are you ready to use your skills to make a global impact? Join Engineers Without Borders for our upcoming general meeting! Whether you're looking to gain hands-on experience, lead a team, or just find a community of like-minded innovators, this is the place to be. 🚀 Major Project Update We’ll be diving into the latest developments on attaining a new project. From technical design 📋 Now Recruiting: Officer Positions We are looking for the next generation of leaders to help drive our mission forward. We have several Officer Positions open and are looking for passionate students to fill them. If you want to play a direct role in shaping the future of our chapter, managing projects, or coordinating outreach, we want to hear from you! Applications and role descriptions will be discussed during the meeting. 🌟 Why Join a Student Organization? Beyond the projects, being part of EWB and the broader student engineering community offers incredible perks: Professional Development: Build your resume with real-world project management and technical design experience. Networking: Connect with university faculty, professional guest speakers, and industry mentors. Leadership Skills: Move beyond the classroom by leading teams and managing budgets. Community: Make lifelong friends who share your passion for civil engineering and global service. 📅 Meeting Details: Date: April 29, 2026 Time: 4:30pm Location: AEB 150 Let’s build something better together. See you there!

name: APIME Mini Con
description: Join us for a half-day experience centered on community-building, history, and challenging dominant narratives through engaging, facilitated conversations. 🗓 Thursday, April 30 ⏰ 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM 📍 Barrick Museum Session 1 | 11:00 AM – 12:15 PM Dreaming Towards a Place for APIME Communities: Between Vision and Action Explore how Asian, Pacific Islander, and Middle Eastern communities have created spaces to thrive through art, activism, and social justice—and imagine what that could look like in Las Vegas. 🍽 Lunch | 12:15 PM – 12:45 PM Take a break, connect with others, and enjoy lunch on us! Session 2 | 12:45 PM – 2:00 PM Not Your Model Minority Unpack the Model Minority Myth, its impact across communities, and how we can challenge it to build more inclusive and honest narratives. ✨ What to Expect: Engaging, thought-provoking conversations Opportunities to reflect and connect A welcoming, community-centered space Drop in anytime—attend one session or stay for both. The Henderson Equality Center food pantry aids the community by providing free, weekly, non-judgmental access to frozen, refrigerated, and dry food. Additionally, the center offers free clothing, hygiene produces, school supplies, and even wellness resources. Please come in comfortable clothing and close toes shoes. We will leave campus by 12:30pm, arrive around 1pm and help for two hours. Food will be provided so keep an eye on your email for more details on that!

We will meet at the Student Union third floor conference room 316 inside the office of Service, Learning, & Leadership at 12:00 PM, we will provide transportation for up to 10 people. We will be back by around 4:00 PM. Bring your SLL punch card to reach the Owala Bottle prize if you have one. Don't worry though: if you don't have one, we'll give you a card! name: The Wildlife Society-Herpetofauna Surveys: Chuckwalla Count at Sloan Canyon
location: Meet at the detention basin at the corner of Democracy Drive and Via Firenze in Henderson, NV.
description: We have an exciting field opportunity with the Nevada Department of Wildlife (NDOW) to assist with chuckwalla surveys at Sloan Canyon National Conservation Area. Chuckwallas are large, herbivorous lizards native to the arid rocky deserts of the southwestern U.S. Join NDOW biologists in the field and gain hands-on experience working with native reptiles while contributing to ongoing wildlife monitoring efforts. Spots are limited and filling quickly: Saturday (May 2): 5 spots remaining Sunday (May 3): 5 spots remaining Event Details Dates: Saturday, May 2 & Sunday, May 3, 2026 Time: 8:00 AM Location: Sloan Canyon National Conservation Area This is an excellent opportunity to: Work directly with NDOW biologists Gain field experience in wildlife surveys Build experience for careers in wildlife biology and conservation Sign up here: https://ndow.galaxydigital. com/need/detail/?need_id= 1214059 Due to limited availability, we encourage you to sign up as soon as possible. We hope to see some of you out there!

name: Leadership Link Up : Spongebob Squarepants - Squidward Energy
description: Some days, you're just not feeling that SpongeBob energy—and that's okay. Leadership doesn’t always mean being hyper-positive. In this real-talk session, we explore what it means to lead while feeling burnt out, frustrated, or low-energy. Participants will: Normalize emotional fluctuations (fatigue, frustration, apathy) in leadership roles. ​ Reflect on how honesty and vulnerability can foster team trust and psychological safety. ​ Identify strategies to recharge while maintaining support for others and sustaining impact. ​

name: Snacks & Skills: Sustaining Yourself in the Work: Preventing Activist Burnout
description: Activism can be deeply rewarding, but it can also take a toll on your mental, emotional, and physical well-being. On June 18, 2025, join us for Snacks and Skills as we explore how to sustain yourself in the long run while preventing burnout. Rooted in the Social Change Wheel 2.0, this session will focus on Community Building as a key strategy for sustaining your energy and passion for social change. You’ll learn how to empower yourself and others through leadership development, build strong community support systems, and engage in meaningful self-care practices to ensure your activism is sustainable and impactful.

name: Leadership Link Up - Feel It to Lead It, First Gen & In Our Feelings
description: What if your emotions were your leadership superpower? Join us for a fun and interactive Leadership Link Up inspired by Inside Out as we explore how emotions shape the way we lead, communicate, and navigate college life. Whether you're feeling Joy, Anxiety, Anger, Sadness, or a little bit of everything, you'll learn how emotional awareness can help you build stronger relationships, make better decisions, and lead with authenticity. Connect with fellow first-generation students, enjoy themed activities, and discover how understanding your emotions can strengthen your leadership journey.

name: Fall 2026 Service Day: Project 150
description: Date: Friday, September 25, 2026 Time: 8:00 AM (Meet at Student Union 208) Service Duration: Approximately 3 hours Dress Code: Long pants and closed-toe shoes required ( no exceptions ) About the Site: Project 150 is a nonprofit organization dedicated to supporting homeless, displaced, and disadvantaged high school students in Southern Nevada. The organization provides free resources such as food, clothing, hygiene items, school supplies, and other essential support to help students stay in school and work toward graduation in a stable and dignified way. Service Activities: Volunteers will assist with indoor organization and preparation projects that support Project 150’s daily operations. Tasks may include sorting and organizing clothing and supplies, assembling hygiene kits, stocking shelves, and preparing items for student distribution. Volunteers may work individually or in small groups and will be assigned specific tasks by site staff. Important Information: This is an indoor service site that involves standing, light lifting, and repetitive tasks. Volunteers should be comfortable working in a warehouse-style environment and following all instructions provided by Project 150 staff to ensure safety and efficiency. Transportation: Limited transportation may be available for select sites, with additional details to come. Carpooling is strongly encouraged to help support smooth transportation for all volunteers.

name: Fall 2026 Service Day: Three Square Food Bank
description: Date: Friday, September 25th, 2026 Time: 8:00 AM (Meet at Student Union 208) Service Duration: Approximately 3 hours Dress Code: Long pants and closed-toe shoes required (no exceptions) About the Site: Three Square Food Bank is Southern Nevada’s only food bank and a vital resource in the fight against hunger in our community. Through food distribution, community partnerships, and advocacy, Three Square works to ensure that individuals and families across the region have consistent access to nutritious food. Service Activities: Volunteers will support warehouse-based food distribution efforts by sorting, packing, and organizing food items. Tasks may include standing for extended periods and light lifting to help prepare food for distribution to community partners. Important Information: This service site is primarily indoors. Volunteers should be comfortable working in a warehouse environment and following food safety guidelines. All volunteers must wear long pants and closed-toe shoes. Transportation: Limited transportation may be available for select sites, with additional details to come. Carpooling is strongly encouraged to help support smooth transportation for all volunteers.

name: Fall 2026 Service Day: Las Vegas Science and Natural History Museum
description: Date: Friday, September 25th, 2026 Time: 8:00 AM (Meet at Student Union 208) Service Duration: Approximately 3 hours Dress Code: Long pants and closed-toe shoes required (no exceptions) About the Site: The Las Vegas Science & Natural History Museum is on a mission to inspire a better understanding and appreciation of the natural world, the sciences, and ourselves through educational exhibits, programming, and research. Service Activities: Volunteers will assist with preparations for DinoBall: Weird Science After Dark, a fundraising gala taking place on September 26, 2026, at the Las Vegas Science & Natural History Museum. Support is needed with event setup, including arranging gallery spaces, moving and positioning tables and décor, preparing exhibit areas for guests, and general museum cleaning and organization before the event. Volunteers may also assist with light logistical tasks to help ensure the museum is guest-ready for the gala. Important Information: Volunteers should be comfortable standing, walking, and performing light to moderate physical activity. Some tasks may involve lifting, carrying, or moving event materials and furniture. Closed-toe shoes are required. Transportation: Limited transportation may be available for select sites, with additional details to come. Carpooling is strongly encouraged to help support smooth transportation for all volunteers.

name: Fall 2026 Service Day: Las Vegas - Clark County Library District
description: Date: Friday, September 25th, 2026 Time: 8:00 AM (Meet at Student Union 208) Service Duration: Approximately 3 hours Dress Code: Long pants and closed-toe shoes required (no exceptions) About the Site: The talented, dedicated staff of the Las Vegas-Clark County Library District is committed to nurturing and elevating the lives of the diverse individuals and groups that we serve, and supporting their aspirations. Service Activities: Help prepare donated books for distribution by sorting, organizing, and moving materials in our warehouse. Your work helps get great books back into the hands of people across Las Vegas. Important Information: This service site is primarily indoors. Volunteers must wear closed-toe shoes and work-appropriate clothing. Please bring a refillable water bottle. Transportation: Limited transportation may be available for select sites, with additional details to come. Carpooling is strongly encouraged to help support smooth transportation for all volunteers.

name: Snacks & Skills: Democracy and Donuts (The Simpsons)
location: Student Union 302M (Office with Red Couches On Your Left After You Get Out The Elevator))
description: Grab a donut and join us for a fun, Simpsons -themed exploration of democracy and civic engagement! This interactive program breaks down how democracy works in everyday life—from direct, representative, and constitutional systems to the many ways students can use their voice for change. Through activities, discussion, and connection, you’ll learn how civic engagement shows up on and off campus and how you can take action using the Social Change Wheel to create meaningful impact in your community. Come for the donuts, stay for the democracy!

name: Coffee with Karly
location: Rebel Grounds in the Hospitality Building
description: Grab a coffee, tea or treat on us and meet Karly at Rebel Grounds Coffee Shop in the Hospitality Building for an informal conversation about leadership, involvement, and finding your place at UNLV. Whether you're looking for ways to get more engaged on campus, explore leadership opportunities, or simply connect with other students, this space is designed for honest conversation, questions, and personal growth. Leave with new ideas, resources, and a clearer path toward making your impact on campus and in the community.

name: Leadership Link Up: Bounce Back Season: Built for This
description: Suit up in your favorite jersey and join us for a high-energy Leadership Link Up focused on what it really means to bounce back. College life comes with wins, losses, setbacks, and comebacks—and resilient leadership is about learning how to keep going, adapt, and grow stronger through it all. Through interactive activities and reflection, we’ll explore how to stay grounded under pressure, learn from challenges, and show up for yourself and others when it matters most. Whether you're on offense or defense in life right now, this space is built for you.

name: Snacks and Skills : Good Trouble, Necessary Trouble
description: Join us for Good Trouble, Necessary Trouble. Themed with The Boondocks, this Snacks and Skills event celebrates Black History Month and the legacy of John Lewis. A powerful panel on Black resistance, civil disobedience, and collective action—connecting past movements to present-day leadership!

name: Leadership Link Up : Recouple with Your Authentic Self
description: Welcome to the villa—but this time, the most important connection is with yourself. Inspired by Love Island, this Leadership Link Up invites you to “recouple” with your authentic self and explore what it means to lead with honesty, self-awareness, and intention. Through interactive activities and reflection, you’ll unpack personal values, recognize what shapes your leadership identity, and practice showing up as your most authentic self in relationships, academics, and leadership spaces. No games—just growth, clarity, and real connection.

name: Leadership Link Up: Wayfinding Women of Color in Leadership
description: Set sail on a leadership journey inspired by Moana as we explore what it means to lead with purpose, identity, and community at the center. This Leadership Link Up highlights the experiences of women of color in leadership and invites participants to reflect on how culture, identity, and lived experiences shape the way we lead and navigate the world. Through interactive discussion and activities, we’ll examine barriers, celebrate strengths, and explore how leadership can be both personal and collective. Come ready to reflect, connect, and chart your own path forward.

name: Snacks and Skills: Captain Your Crew (Spongebob)
description: Who’s ready to lead their crew under the sea? Join us for a SpongeBob -themed Snacks & Skills where we explore what it means to step up, get involved, and lead with confidence. Through interactive activities, teamwork challenges, and reflection, you’ll learn how to navigate leadership roles, build your crew of support, and stay engaged in meaningful campus involvement. Whether you’re new to leadership or ready to level up, this session will help you chart your course toward impact and involvement.
